ibmsmooth 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
    • IBM smoothing with INLA
    • Prototype: marginalized Gaussian horseshoe IBM
    • IBM smoothing with Stan

Unified interface for integrated Brownian motion smoothing

Source: R/ibm.R
ibm.Rd

Fit an integrated Brownian motion smoother using either Stan or INLA.

Usage

ibm(
  t,
  y,
  infer_at = NULL,
  method = c("stan", "inla"),
  adaptive = FALSE,
  stan_adaptive_method = c("horseshoe", "rw", "rhs", "bridge"),
  stan_horseshoe_engine = c("joint", "marginalized"),
  ...
)

Arguments

t

Numeric vector of time points.

y

Numeric vector of observations at times t.

infer_at

Optional numeric vector of additional time points at which inference should be returned.

method

Computational backend, either "stan" or "inla".

adaptive

Logical. If FALSE, use a single smoothness parameter. If TRUE, fit a locally adaptive model.

stan_adaptive_method

Stan-only adaptive prior. The default is "horseshoe".

stan_horseshoe_engine

Stan engine used for the adaptive horseshoe model. "joint" retains the existing sampler; "marginalized" uses the Gaussian-data Kalman marginalization and a simulation smoother.

...

Additional arguments passed to ibm_smooth() or ibm_inla_fit().

Value

An object of class ibmfit.
